Disease.description: A medical condition affecting the human body

Treatment.description: A medical intervention used to manage or cure a disease

Biomarker.description: A measurable indicator of a biological state or condition

DiagnosticMethod.description: A procedure or test used to diagnose a disease

RiskFactor.description: A factor that increases the likelihood of developing a disease

ClinicalOutcome.description: A measurable result of a treatment or disease progression

Lutein.description: A hydroxycarotenoid found in the diet

Zeaxanthin.description: A hydroxycarotenoid found in the diet

MacularPigment.description: An accumulation of lutein and zeaxanthin in the retina

SubfovealChoroidalNeovascularization.description: A type of choroidal neovascularization that affects the subfoveal region of the eye

OcularHistoplasmosisSyndrome.description: A disease caused by the histoplasmosis fungus that affects the eyes

IdiopathicChoroidalNeovascularization.description: A type of choroidal neovascularization with no known cause

SubmacularSurgery.description: A surgical procedure to remove subfoveal choroidal neovascularization

CorticosteroidTreatment.description: A treatment using corticosteroids to reduce inflammation

Carotenoid.description: A class of biomarkers that include lutein, vitamin C, vitamin E, and beta-carotene

FluocinoloneAcetonide.description: A type of corticosteroid used to treat age-related macular degeneration

FeederVessels.description: Abnormal blood vessels that feed choroidal neovascularization

Cyclooxygenase2Inhibition.description: A type of treatment that inhibits cyclooxygenase-2

ChoroidalNeovascularization.description: A disease characterized by the growth of new blood vessels in the choroid

NeovascularAgeRelatedMacularDegeneration.description: A type of age-related macular degeneration characterized by the growth of new blood vessels

OcularTumor.description: Tumors of the iris and ciliary body

AmishCommunity.description: A population isolate in the Lancaster and Franklin counties area in Pennsylvania

LateARM.description: A late stage of age-related macular degeneration

AtrophicAgeRelatedMacularDegeneration.description: A type of Age-Related Macular Degeneration characterized by atrophy

Antioxidant.description: A substance that prevents or reduces cell damage caused by reactive oxygen species

OculomotorTraining.description: A type of training for patients with macular disease

UnilateralChoroidalNeovascularisation.description: A condition where choroidal neovascularisation occurs in one eye.

NeovascularMaculopathy.description: A condition characterised by the growth of new blood vessels in the macula.

SerousDetachment.description: A condition where fluid accumulates under the retina.

PulsatileOcularBloodFlow.description: A type of blood flow in the eye

ReadingTraining.description: A treatment to help improve reading ability in people with visual impairment

PhotodynamicTherapy.description: A type of treatment that uses light to destroy abnormal blood vessels

AgeRelatedMaculopathy.description: degeneration or atrophy of the macula - the center part of the retina responsible for fine vision

inhibits.description: A treatment that inhibits a molecular target
